overview: Refactor the internal resource service to improve readability and maintainability while preserving current deploy, delete, and deployment-confirmation behavior across supported Camunda versions. Keep the work within the existing API, factory, and versioned-service structure, review generated-client coverage explicitly, and add a bounded missing capability only if it is cross-version, low risk, and fully verifiable.

userStories.title: Lock the shared resource service contract
userStories.description: Define and stabilize the shared resource service surface, version routing expectations, and generated-client contract boundaries before refactoring the implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/services/resource/api.go` defines the preserved shared service surface for the refactor.
`internal/services/resource/factory_test.go` verifies supported version routing and unsupported version failure behavior.
`internal/services/resource/v87/contract.go` and `internal/services/resource/v88/contract.go` match the generated-client operations required by the settled service surface.
userStories.notes: Start here because later refactor and capability decisions depend on a stable shared API and contract boundary.

userStories.title: Refactor v8.7 resource behavior without regression
userStories.description: Simplify the v8.7 resource service implementation so deploy and delete flows are easier to maintain while keeping observable behavior unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/services/resource/v87/service.go` preserves current deploy and delete behavior for existing callers.
Success-path payload validation uses repository-native patterns and still rejects malformed success responses.
`internal/services/resource/v87/service_test.go` covers preserved deploy, delete, and malformed-response behavior.
userStories.notes: Keep this slice focused on v8.7 behavior preservation plus adjacent tests; do not bundle the capability-review work into this story.

userStories.title: Refactor v8.8 resource behavior without regression
userStories.description: Simplify the v8.8 resource service implementation while preserving current deploy, delete, and wait-for-confirmation behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/services/resource/v88/service.go` preserves deploy, delete, and `--no-wait` versus confirmation-poll behavior.
Shared payload validation and response handling are clearer without changing current success or failure semantics.
`internal/services/resource/v88/service_test.go` covers deploy, delete, malformed-success, and confirmation-path behavior.
userStories.notes: Preserve the current confirmation poll semantics and avoid normalizing behavior differences just because both versions are being cleaned up.

userStories.title: Complete the generated-client coverage decision
userStories.description: Review the supported generated resource clients and either expose one bounded missing capability or explicitly record a no-addition decision.
acceptanceCriteria: The final generated-client coverage review is recorded in `specs/71-resource-api-refactor/research.md`.
Any added capability is available in both supported versions, fits the existing service surface, and does not require package or layout changes.
If no capability is added, the bounded rationale is recorded explicitly.
The shared service API and both versioned contracts and services reflect the final add-or-defer decision consistently.
userStories.notes: Resource metadata lookup is the leading candidate; raw content retrieval is lower priority unless it can be kept equally small and verifiable.

userStories.title: Prove completion with durable regression coverage
userStories.description: Expand and tighten regression coverage so future maintainers can safely modify the resource service without rediscovering hidden behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Resource service tests cover preserved success paths, malformed-success handling, edge-case delete behavior, and version-specific differences.
`specs/71-resource-api-refactor/quickstart.md` records the final validation sequence and documentation-impact decision.
`go test ./internal/services/resource/... -race -count=1` passes.
`make test` passes.
userStories.notes: This story should consolidate the final regression matrix and repository-required proof of completion after the service shape has settled.

userStories.title: Handle documentation impact only if behavior becomes user-visible
userStories.description: Keep the feature internal-only by default, but update user-facing documentation in the same change if the final capability decision affects operator-visible workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: If no user-visible workflow changes, the feature artifacts explicitly record that documentation remains unchanged.
If a user-visible workflow changes, `README.md` and affected generated CLI docs under `docs/cli/` are updated in the same change.
The final validation path makes the documentation decision explicit.
userStories.notes: Keep this as a small final slice because the documentation work is conditional on the outcome of the generated-client coverage decision.
